query: SELECT id, meetingTitle, meetingDate, executiveSummary, sourceType, sourceId, createdBy FROM meetings WHERE id = 1200001
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway04.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2U3q1dt8GKGPLLB.root --database WN9ut9YEufdDjevBezkAVQ --execute SELECT id, meetingTitle, meetingDate, executiveSummary, sourceType, sourceId, createdBy FROM meetings WHERE id = 1200001
messages: 1200001	03-07 Manus-Centric Automation for Transcript Summarization and Identity Resolution with Manual Approval for New Contacts	2026-03-07 14:09:41	## Core Synopsis

The recording-to-summary pipeline is bifurcated between a Pod app path that generates transcript and summary with manual speaker naming, and a Manus-centric path that ingests raw transcripts for LLM-driven summarization, speaker identification, and auto-tagging to contacts with a manual approval gate for net-new clients; the leverage sits in Manus-side automation that resolves speakers and routes artifacts without human intervention, while preserving a human-in-the-loop only for new contact creation to prevent database pollution. The transcript confirms the Pod app currently produces a usable summary but forces manual speaker mapping, whereas the proposed Manus ingestion would infer identities and auto-assign records, then flag unknown names as “new client” for approver validation. We are accepting manual tagging for now while we design Manus-side automation because speed-to-stability matters more than premature complexity; the stakes are operational efficiency and data integrity: automating identity resolution reduces repetitive admin work, and keeping an approval step for new contacts prevents mis-tagging and orphan records.
- Recording is initiated and stopped on the Pod device; stopping sends the transcript to the Pod app.
- The Pod app can auto-generate a full transcript and summary, then prompt the user to manually assign speaker names (e.g., “Speaker 1 = Kyle,” “Speaker 2 = Janae,” etc.).
- This path produces usable artifacts but relies on manual speaker labeling before downstream tagging.
- Raw transcripts would be sent directly to Manus.
- An LLM would perform end-to-end processing: generate the summary, infer speakers’ names, and auto-tag notes to the correct contact records.
- Unknown names would be marked as “new client,” triggering a manual approval step to add them to the contact list.
- Identity resolution shifts from manual speaker mapping in the Pod app to automated entity recognition in Manus.
- The approval gate for “new client” creation remains intentionally manual to protect contact integrity and avoid false positives.
- Status quo remains: manual speaker naming via the Pod app is acceptable in the short term.
- Future target: Manus-side ingestion and automated identity resolution, with a retained manual approval gate for new contacts to balance efficiency and control.

1200001	03-07 Manus-Centric Automation for Transcript Summarization and Identity Resolution with Manual Approval for New Contacts	2026-03-07 14:09:41	## Core Synopsis
The recording-to-summary pipeline is bifurcated between a Pod app path that generates transcript and summary with manual speaker naming, and a Manus-centric path that ingests raw transcripts for LLM-driven summarization, speaker identification, and auto-tagging to contacts with a manual approval gate for net-new clients; the leverage sits in Manus-side automation that resolves speakers and routes artifacts without human intervention, while preserving a human-in-the-loop only for new contact creation to prevent database pollution. The transcript confirms the Pod app currently produces a usable summary but forces manual speaker mapping, whereas the proposed Manus ingestion would infer identities and auto-assign records, then flag unknown names as “new client” for approver validation. We are accepting manual tagging for now while we design Manus-side automation because speed-to-stability matters more than premature complexity; the stakes are operational efficiency and data integrity: automating identity resolution reduces repetitive admin work, and keeping an approval step for new contacts prevents mis-tagging and orphan records.

## Workflow Architecture and Decision Logic

### The Current Capture-to-Summary Workflow
- Recording is initiated and stopped on the Pod device; stopping sends the transcript to the Pod app.
- The Pod app can auto-generate a full transcript and summary, then prompt the user to manually assign speaker names (e.g., “Speaker 1 = Kyle,” “Speaker 2 = Janae,” etc.).
- This path produces usable artifacts but relies on manual speaker labeling before downstream tagging.

### The Automation Proposal: Manus-Centric Ingestion
- Raw transcripts would be sent directly to Manus.
- An LLM would perform end-to-end processing: generate the summary, infer speakers’ names, and auto-tag notes to the correct contact records.
- Unknown names would be marked as “new client,” triggering a manual approval step to add them to the contact list.

### Identity Resolution and Contact Approval
- Identity resolution shifts from manual speaker mapping in the Pod app to automated entity recognition in Manus.
- The approval gate for “new client” creation remains intentionally manual to protect contact integrity and avoid false positives.

### The Interim Operating Decision
- Status quo remains: manual speaker naming via the Pod app is acceptable in the short term.
- Future target: Manus-side ingestion and automated identity resolution, with a retained manual approval gate for new contacts to balance efficiency and control.
